growth_records.medium: ASW-YT (artificial sea-water with yeast extract and tryptone) supplemented with agmatine
growth_records.cite: Fluke et al. The extensive m5C epitranscriptome of Thermococcus kodakarensis is generated by a suite of RNA methyltransferases that support thermophily. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-51410-w. [PMC11344067]
growth_records.snippet: T. kodakarensis grows over a wide temperature range (~50 to ~98 °C) with a growth optimum of 85 °C resulting in a ~40-min doubling time under ideal conditions.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (~40 min = 0.667 h) at the 85°C growth optimum is stated directly in the paper's own text as a general characterization of T. kodakarensis (parental strain TS559, grown anaerobically in ASW-YT+agmatine per this paper's Methods), but is given as an approximate figure without SD/replicate count or an explicit citation in that sentence, so treated as reported (not a fitted/calculated value from a specific dataset in this paper) with medium confidence. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/0.667h = 1.04 h-1 for convenience; input number used: doubling time = 40 min stated in paper.

growth_records.strain: TKAg (TS559 background carrying empty vector, control)
growth_records.medium: not explicitly specified for this growth-curve assay (routine liquid cultivation of T. kodakarensis in this study used rich medium ASW-YT or synthetic medium ASW-AAW, under strict anaerobic/N2 atmosphere with Na2S reduction)
growth_records.cite: Villain et al. The hyperthermophilic archaeon Thermococcus kodakarensis is resistant to pervasive negative supercoiling activity of DNA gyrase. Nucleic Acids Research (2021). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkab869. [PMC8643681]
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth rates (number of divisions h-1) of the control (TKY119, TKAg) and gyrase expressing strain TKgyrAB) were 2.11 ± 0.27 h-1 TKAg, 1.45 ± 0.35 h-1 TKY119 and 1.81 ± 0.37 h-1 TKgyrAB.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate (2.11 ± 0.27 h-1) is explicitly reported for the empty-vector control strain TKAg, monitored by direct cell counting in batch culture under the archaeon's optimal growth conditions (T. kodakarensis optimal growth temperature stated elsewhere in paper as 85 degC; all cultivation of this strain in the study is strictly anaerobic). doubling_time_h calculated as ln(2)/2.11 = 0.328 h. Medium used specifically for this growth-curve figure (Fig. 6A) is not named in the results text; ASW-YT/ASW-AAW are the two media used for routine cultivation elsewhere in Methods, hence medium confidence is reduced to medium rather than high.

growth_records.strain: TKY119F (TS559 background expressing catalytically dead gyrase Y119F mutant, control)
growth_records.medium: not explicitly specified for this growth-curve assay (routine liquid cultivation of T. kodakarensis in this study used rich medium ASW-YT or synthetic medium ASW-AAW, under strict anaerobic/N2 atmosphere with Na2S reduction)
growth_records.cite: Villain et al. The hyperthermophilic archaeon Thermococcus kodakarensis is resistant to pervasive negative supercoiling activity of DNA gyrase. Nucleic Acids Research (2021). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkab869. [PMC8643681]
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth rates (number of divisions h-1) of the control (TKY119, TKAg) and gyrase expressing strain TKgyrAB) were 2.11 ± 0.27 h-1 TKAg, 1.45 ± 0.35 h-1 TKY119 and 1.81 ± 0.37 h-1 TKgyrAB.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate (1.45 ± 0.35 h-1) explicitly reported for strain referred to in-text as 'TKY119' which corresponds to the catalytically dead gyrase point-mutant control strain TKY119F (tyrosine 119 to phenylalanine, unable to introduce negative supercoiling) defined earlier in the Methods. doubling_time_h = ln(2)/1.45 = 0.478 h. Same batch-culture/optimal-growth-condition context (85 degC, anaerobic) as the other two strains in this figure; specific medium for this assay not named in the results text.

growth_records.strain: TKgyrAB (TS559 background expressing active Thermotoga maritima gyrase gyrA/gyrB operon)
growth_records.medium: not explicitly specified for this growth-curve assay (routine liquid cultivation of T. kodakarensis in this study used rich medium ASW-YT or synthetic medium ASW-AAW, under strict anaerobic/N2 atmosphere with Na2S reduction)
growth_records.cite: Villain et al. The hyperthermophilic archaeon Thermococcus kodakarensis is resistant to pervasive negative supercoiling activity of DNA gyrase. Nucleic Acids Research (2021). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkab869. [PMC8643681]
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth rates (number of divisions h-1) of the control (TKY119, TKAg) and gyrase expressing strain TKgyrAB) were 2.11 ± 0.27 h-1 TKAg, 1.45 ± 0.35 h-1 TKY119 and 1.81 ± 0.37 h-1 TKgyrAB.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate (1.81 ± 0.37 h-1) explicitly reported for the T. maritima gyrase-expressing strain TKgyrAB, in which plasmid DNA is converted from positively to strongly negatively supercoiled. doubling_time_h = ln(2)/1.81 = 0.383 h. The paper states the differences in slopes between the three strains (TKAg, TKY119F, TKgyrAB) were not statistically significant (P = 0.34, ANCOVA), i.e., gyrase expression did not measurably affect growth rate. Same batch/optimal-growth-condition context (85 degC, anaerobic) as the other two strains; specific medium for this assay not named in the results text.
